howItWorks.intro: This cutting calorie calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Protein = 1.2g per pound bodyweight during a cut
howItWorks.outro: Individual results vary based on metabolism, genetics, and activity level. Consult your doctor before starting any program.

faq.question: How big of a deficit for cutting?
faq.answer: 500 cal/day deficit for 1 lb/week loss. Aggressive cuts (750-1000) lose faster but risk muscle loss. Never go below BMR.

faq.question: How much protein when cutting?
faq.answer: Higher than bulking: 1.1-1.3g per pound bodyweight. Extra protein preserves muscle mass during a caloric deficit.

faq.question: How long should I cut?
faq.answer: Until reaching 10-12% body fat (men) or 18-22% (women). Typical cuts last 8-16 weeks. Take diet breaks every 6-8 weeks if needed.

faq.question: Will I lose muscle?
faq.answer: Some muscle loss is inevitable, but high protein, heavy lifting, and moderate deficit minimize it. Crash dieting causes significant muscle loss.

faq.question: Is the Cutting Calorie Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Cutting Calorie Calculator?
sections.body: The Cutting Calorie Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes and fitness enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the fitness and training space. By entering your weight, height, age, you get instant results including maintenance calories, cut calories, protein. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting maintenance calories right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In fitness and training, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes and fitness enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to optimize your training with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your weight and need to find the right maintenance calories. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Weight (lbs)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your weight — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: height, age, activity. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your maintenance calories, cut calories, protein. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your weight before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both maintenance calories and cut calories — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to optimize your training.
If you're unsure about your activity, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
